title: Locale route renders hard-coded English content

evidence.quote:
Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about EscrowHubs on BlockDAG.

reasoning: The page lives under the locale segment and the app is configured with next-intl, but this route does not read the locale or use translations for either the page chrome or the FAQ data. As a result, non-English locale URLs such as /es/learn/faq will still render English strings, which breaks the locale route contract for user-visible content.
reproduction: Open two locale variants, for example /en/learn/faq and /es/learn/faq. The heading, description, and FAQ entries are rendered from the same hard-coded English strings/data instead of locale-specific messages.
recommendation: Move the FAQ heading, description, and FAQ item copy into next-intl messages and render them through the locale-aware translation API used elsewhere in the frontend.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are linked for this feature, and the route has no coverage asserting that different locale paths render localized FAQ content.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a route-level rendering test that loads at least two supported locales and asserts the FAQ heading and one FAQ answer come from the locale-specific message catalog.
minimumFixScope: Update frontend/src/app/[locale]/learn/faq/page.tsx and the relevant message/mock-data source so FAQ content is resolved per locale.
